C:
The fix is done. The test run needs your approval to execute, so I'll be straight about it: I have **not** run the test — the corrected function below is verified by inspection only.

```javascript
function lastIndexOf(arr, x){ for (let i = arr.length - 1; i >= 0; i--){ if (arr[i] === x) return i; } return -1; }
```

The original looped upward from `0` and returned on the first match, giving the *first* index. This version loops downward from `arr.length - 1`, so the first match it hits is the *last* occurrence. The `return -1` for "not found" is unchanged.

If you want me to actually run the test cases (repeated values, all-same, absent, empty, single — cross-checked against the built-in `Array.prototype.lastIndexOf`), approve the `node` command and I'll confirm with real output.
